question: Why is the architecture structured this way?
title: Architecture evolution
summary: A deliberately boring stack—documented at each stage so “simplify” and “add auth” prompts do not erase intent.

scenes.label: Architecture snapshots
scenes.description: The system did not jump to its current shape. Each snapshot reflects what existed—and what was recorded—at that time.

snapshots.title: Intent only
snapshots.body: PHP on cPanel, SQLite file, server-rendered templates. No framework. Documented before implementation so AI sessions did not import Laravel by default.
changes: Database as a file beside the app
No build step on shared hosting
snapshots.note: This snapshot exists in project.json before the ideas table was created.

snapshots.title: Thin vertical slice
snapshots.body: One ideas module: list, create, edit, delete. Includes layer for DB, simple validation, PHP templates. No service container, no ORM.

snapshots.title: Fields grow, boundaries hold
snapshots.body: Status and sort_order added. Architecture unchanged: still one operator, still SQLite, still no auth layer. Boundaries reinforced in warnings/ not in code comments alone.
snapshots.note: Originally we planned MySQL for “serious” hosting. We stayed on SQLite because the operator count did not change.

snapshots.title: Stable mental model
snapshots.body: Three layers: templates (views), ideas handlers (CRUD), database file (truth). Edition documents modules; guide explains why the layers must not collapse.
changes: Debugging guides for deploy vs local drift

scenes.label: Current system picture
scenes.description: Select a layer. Each explanation includes what breaks if you treat this as optional.

layers.title: One operator
layers.detail: Authentication was rejected for v1—not deferred. Adding auth halfway is a documented trap. See decisions/no-authentication.md.

layers.title: PHP templates
layers.detail: No SPA. Keeps deploy simple on cPanel. AI must not introduce a build pipeline without a recorded decision.

layers.title: Ideas handlers
layers.detail: Form names, validation, write, and read must stay aligned. This invariant saved the project twice.

layers.title: SQLite file
layers.detail: Not deployed via rsync—by design. Production DB is persistent on the host. Blank list debugging starts here.

cards.title: cPanel shared hosting
cards.teaser: No Node build, no background workers.

cards.title: Single operator
cards.teaser: Collaboration is out of scope until explicitly chosen.
points: Multi-user requires a new decision set

cards.title: Plain text ideas
points: Attachments would change hosting and security model
